$comment: Self-derived finetype drift contract for source.naics (the finetype_validate@1 gate checks the terminal Parquet against this in CHECK-ONLY mode). Regenerate with finetype (>=0.6.50): finetype profile -f build/naics.parquet -o json-schema --stats | jq '{"$comment":...} + del(.properties[].pattern)'. Rationale: the per-type canonical 'pattern' regexes finetype emits encode idealised formats that legitimately-varied production data violates — here finetype's 'code' NAICS pattern rejects the official hyphenated sector ranges (31-33, 44-45, 48-49). The gate therefore enforces the OBSERVABLE envelope finetype measured — type, minLength/maxLength, numeric minimum/maximum, closed enum domains, and the x-finetype-* semantic labels — not the format ideal. So it is a pass-through on today's Run and a tripwire for future drift.

corpus.x-finetype-unknown-reason: validation rejected 'alphanumeric_id': only 21% of values matched its format

description.x-finetype-unknown-reason: validation rejected 'alphanumeric_id': only 26% of values matched its format

title: naics
